Summary
Microsoft Certified Professional with 11 years of extensive experience in database administration, specializing in MS SQL
Server versions 2016/2014/2012/2008 R2/2008. Proven expertise in managing complex database environments,
implementing high availability solutions, and optimizing performance. Skilled in incident, change, and problem
management within multi-client data center services. Possesses a solid understanding of Microsoft Azure services and
related database technologies.
Education
Indira College, Pune University
Masters of Science, Computer Science 2011, Pune, Maharashtra, India
Experience
Senior Database Administrator On-Site
Ensono Aug 2023 - Present, Bangalore, India
•Assumed responsibilities as Senior Database Administrator following an employment transfer as part of a project
acquisition from Wipro.
•Continuously provided expert database administration and support for critical systems.
Lead Database Administrator On-Site
Wipro Technologies Aug 2018 - Jul 2023, Bangalore, India
•Provided 11 years of overall experience in database administration, specializing in MS SQL Server
2016/2014/2012/2008 R2/2008.
•Managed MS SQL Server installations, upgrades, configurations, and ongoing administration across various
versions.
•Maintained Microsoft Azure certified status with a strong understanding of Azure VM, Azure Storage, Azure
Network, Azure AD, and Azure SQL database.
•Spearheaded incident management, change management, and problem management for the SQL Server Database
team.
•Participated in crucial client meetings and conference calls with Service Delivery Managers and Application teams
for system improvements.
•Conducted quarterly Disaster Recovery (DR) activities and contributed to the creation of Service Improvement
Plans (SIP).
•Handled high severity issues, providing timely Root Cause Analysis (RCA) to prevent recurrence.
•Worked extensively with high availability solutions including Always On Availability Groups, database mirroring,
replication, clustering, and log shipping.
•Provided essential on-call support during out-of-office hours and weekends to ensure continuous service
availability.
•Managed resources and shifts for a team of 5 SQL DBAs from offshore in a multi-client data center environment.
•Provided Knowledge Transfer (KT) to team members, actively monitoring and guiding trainees.
•Participated in PCI scan reports, performing installations of security hot fixes and service packs to remove SQL
server vulnerabilities.
•Involved in Holmes BOTS automation implementation for the SQL Pack tool.
•Handled service requests, incidents, and critical i

ssues, providing prompt DBA support during conference calls.
•Executed performance tuning to optimize database efficiency and responsiveness.
•Environment: SQL Server 2016/2014/2012/2008R2/2008, Windows Server 2012/2008R2/2008.
Database Administrator On-Site
Mphasis Aug 2016 - Jul 2018, Bangalore, India

•Managed MS SQL Server 2012/2008 R2/2008 installation, configuration, and administration.
•Worked on Always On Availability Groups, log shipping, database mirroring, and clustering solutions.
•Performed regular SQL server patching activities to ensure system security and stability.
•Generated daily reports including cluster failover, backup, AG/LS/Mirror status, and server disk space.
•Handled service requests, incidents, and critical issues, ensuring prompt resolution.
•Participated in quarterly Disaster Recovery (DR) activities, contributing to business continuity planning.
•Provided DBA support for critical issues during conference calls.
•Supported the Windows team during patching for AG/mirror/cluster failover/failback and database health checks.
•Performed all health checks for market open servers and provided updates in market open calls.
•Deeply involved in issue resolution and finding the root cause analysis of issues.
•Performed performance tuning to enhance database operations.
•Environment: SQL Server 2012/2008R2/2008, Windows Server 2008R2/2008.
Database Administrator / Trainee On-Site
Synechron Technologies Pvt. Ltd Aug 2013 - Jul 2016, Pune, India
•Provided support tasks for SQL Server, Oracle, and Netezza databases.
•Managed MS SQL Server 2008 R2/2008 installation, upgrade, and administration.
•Conducted capacity planning for database growth across all SQL servers.
•Troubleshot alerts and worked on log shipping and mirroring implementations.
•Pursued in-depth training on Oracle 11g Architecture and SQL Server.
•Carried out version changes for schemas from PE8 to EE11 as per defined processes.
•Environment: SQL Server 2008R2/2008, Windows Server 2008R2/2008, Oracle 10g/RAC, Oracle 11g.
Database Administrator On-Site
Mastek Ltd Aug 2012 - Jul 2013, Mumbai, India

•Performed database backup/restore operations.
•Carried out MS SQL Server audits.
•Worked with database mirroring, replication, log shipping, and clustering.
•Supported User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and Production environments.
•Reviewed deployment documents and carried out deployments for different applications.
•Environment: SQL Server 2008R2/2008, Windows Server 2008R2/2008.
Software Developer / ERP Customizer On-Site
PP Software and Systems Ltd Aug 2011 - Jul 2012, Pune, India
•Designed, coded, and tested modules for a system providing Master Data Management and Procurement for the
dairy industry.
•C

ustomized ERP system as per client requirements, ensuring functional specifications were met.
•Environment: Core Java, PostgreSQL.
Projects
Certificates

Microsoft Azure Certified – Issued: Circa 2019

Operating Systems: MS Windows Server 2012 - 2008 - XP
Databases: MS SQL Server 2016 - 2014 - 2012 - 2008 R2 - 2008, Oracle 10g - 11g, Netezza, PostgreSQL
Cloud Platforms: Microsoft Azure (VM, Storage, Network, AD, SQL Database)
Database Management: Always On Availability Group, Database Mirroring, Performance Tuning, Log
Shipping, Installation, Upgrade, Migration, Patching, Replication, Clustering, Backup/Restore, Capacity
Planning, Auditing, Incident Management, Change Management, Problem Management

Diagnostic Manager, Remedy, Jira, Infopacc, Tivoli TDP backup tool, SQL Pack

Mostafa Gamal is a Microsoft Certified Professional with 11 years of experience in database administration, specializing in MS SQL Server. He has expertise in managing complex database environments and implementing high availability solutions.
Mostafa Gamal obtained a Masters of Science in Computer Science from Indira College, Pune University in 2011, located in Pune, Maharashtra, India.

From August 2016 to July 2018, the individual worked as a Database Administrator at Mphasis in Bangalore, India, providing DBA support, executing performance tuning, and managing SQL Server and Windows Server environments.

As a Database Administrator at Mphasis from August 2016 to July 2018, the individual managed SQL Server installations, worked with Always On Availability Groups and other high availability solutions, performed patching, generated reports, handled incidents, and participated in Disaster Recovery activities.

From August 2013 to July 2016, the individual worked at Synechron Technologies Pvt. Ltd in Pune, India, providing support for SQL Server, Oracle, and Netezza databases, managing SQL Server installations, conducting capacity planning, and troubleshooting alerts.

While at Synechron Technologies Pvt. Ltd, the individual pursued training on Oracle 11g and SQL Server, carried out version changes, and worked with SQL Server 2008R2/2008, Windows Server 2008R2/2008, Oracle 10g/RAC, and Oracle 11g.

From August 2012 to July 2013, the individual worked at Mastek Ltd in Mumbai, India, managing SQL Server installations, performing backups and audits, and working with database mirroring, replication, and log shipping.

From August 2011 to July 2012, the individual worked at PP Software and Systems Ltd in Pune, India, designing, coding, and testing modules for a Master Data Management and Procurement system for the dairy industry.
The individual developed a customized ERP system based on client needs, ensuring all functional specifications were fulfilled, utilizing Core Java and PostgreSQL.
The individual holds a Microsoft Certified Professional (MCP) certification issued around 2012 and a Microsoft Azure Certified certification issued around 2019.
The individual possesses skills in operating systems including MS Windows Server 2012, 2008, and XP.
The individual is proficient in various databases such as MS SQL Server (versions 2016, 2014, 2012, 2008 R2, 2008), Oracle 10g and 11g, Netezza, and PostgreSQL.
The individual has experience with Microsoft Azure cloud platform services including VM, Storage, Network, AD, and SQL Database.

The individual is skilled in database management tasks such as Always On Availability Group, Database Mirroring, Performance Tuning, Log Shipping, Installation, Upgrade, Migration, Patching, Replication, Clustering, Backup/Restore, Capacity Planning, Auditing, Incident Management, Change Management, and Problem Management.

The individual is familiar with tools and software including MS LiteSpeed, Idera SQL Safe, SSMS, Upgrade Advisor, SQL Server Profiler, SCOM, Diagnostic Manager, Remedy, Jira, Infopacc, Tivoli TDP backup tool, and SQL Pack.
From August 2023 to the present, Mostafa Gamal has been working as a Senior Database Administrator at Ensono in Bangalore, India, following a transfer from Wipro as part of a project acquisition.
From August 2018 to July 2023, Mostafa Gamal worked as a Lead Database Administrator at Wipro Technologies in Bangalore, India, with 11 years of overall experience in database administration.

While at Wipro, Mostafa Gamal managed MS SQL Server installations, maintained Microsoft Azure certifications, spearheaded incident and change management, participated in client meetings, conducted DR activities, handled high severity issues, and worked with high availability solutions.
Mostafa Gamal managed a team of 5 SQL DBAs, provided knowledge transfer, participated in PCI scans, and was involved in Holmes BOTS automation implementation.

Disaster Recovery, as experienced by Mostafa Gamal, isn’t a theoretical exercise, but a practiced skillset honed across enterprise-level environments. It’s a proactive stance against data loss, a meticulous choreography of systems designed to ensure business continuity when the unexpected strikes. At Mphasis and Wipro, Gamal wasn’t simply *involved* in DR; he actively *conducted* it, building upon a strong foundation in managing and maintaining the very systems it protects – specifically, MS SQL Server installations. 

He’s fluent in the language of resilience, comfortable deploying and managing high availability solutions like SQL Server’s Always On Availability Groups and leveraging the scalability of Microsoft Azure. This isn’t just about keeping servers running; it’s about anticipating failure, architecting redundancy, and swiftly restoring operations. Gamal’s role extends beyond technical implementation to encompass the entire lifecycle – from patching and incident handling to generating crucial reports that illuminate system health and inform recovery strategies. He’s a troubleshooter capable of tackling high-severity issues, a communicator comfortable in client meetings, and a meticulous manager of both incident and change, all while keeping the ultimate goal – a seamless recovery – firmly in sight. Disaster Recovery, in his hands, is a blend of technical expertise, strategic foresight, and calm, decisive action.

Bangalore. A city that hums with the quiet energy of data, a magnet for those who speak the language of servers and SQL. It’s a place where careers are built and reshaped, often within the sprawling campuses of tech giants. For Mostafa Gamal, Bangalore represents a consistent thread in a decade-plus career as a Database Administrator. He first arrived in 2016, finding a foothold at Mphasis, honing his skills in performance tuning and server management. A five-year stretch at Wipro Technologies followed, seeing him rise to a Lead role, a testament to his expertise. Now, as of 2023, he’s settled at Ensono, a move born from a project acquisition – a common story in this dynamic city. Bangalore isn’t merely a location for Gamal; it’s a professional ecosystem, a place where companies like Wipro and Ensono thrive, and where a DBA’s experience is constantly in demand. It’s a city that absorbs talent and offers opportunity, a digital heart beating steadily in the landscape of India.

PostgreSQL: a robust and open-source relational database management system, quietly powering the backbone of bespoke solutions. Not flashy, but relentlessly dependable, it’s a workhorse favored by developers who demand control and scalability. While comfortable alongside industry giants like MS SQL Server and Oracle, PostgreSQL distinguishes itself with its adherence to standards and powerful extensibility. It’s the foundation upon which tailored systems are built – evidenced by its role in a recent, fully customized ERP system, meticulously crafted to meet specific client requirements. It doesn’t simply *store* data; it *supports* complex business logic, proving itself a versatile partner in projects demanding precision and adaptability. A cornerstone of the databases landscape, PostgreSQL is a testament to the power of open-source reliability.

MS SQL Server 2012 is a robust and reliable relational database management system, a cornerstone of data storage and retrieval. Though now succeeded by newer iterations, it remains a capable workhorse, particularly valued for its stability and feature set. It’s a platform demanding skilled stewardship – as evidenced by its presence within the infrastructure managed at Mphasis, where a Database Administrator from 2016-2018 oversaw its health, security, and performance. This included implementing high availability solutions like Always On Availability Groups, ensuring minimal downtime, and diligently applying patches to maintain integrity. 

SQL Server 2012 isn’t a solitary entity; it exists within a broader family of database technologies. The administrator’s proficiency extends beyond it to encompass newer versions like 2016 and 2014, alongside older systems like 2008 R2 and 2008, as well as Oracle, Netezza, and PostgreSQL – suggesting a need for interoperability and a comprehensive understanding of data management principles. Its role at Mphasis wasn’t simply about keeping the system running, but about safeguarding critical data through incident handling and participation in comprehensive Disaster Recovery planning. It’s a mature, proven database, capable of handling significant workloads when properly maintained, and a key component in the data ecosystems of organizations like Mphasis.

SQL Server 2008 is a robust and mature relational database management system, a workhorse quietly powering critical operations for numerous organizations. Though now superseded by newer iterations, it remains a significant presence in established IT landscapes. It’s a platform demanding skilled custodians – Database Administrators like those at Mphasis and Mastek Ltd, who expertly managed its installations, meticulously performed backups and audits, and leveraged features like database mirroring, replication, and log shipping to ensure data integrity and availability. 

Its strength lies in its reliability and established feature set, making it a cornerstone for applications requiring stable data storage and retrieval. Professionals at Synechron Technologies honed their skills alongside it, navigating version changes and integrating it within broader environments encompassing Oracle databases and Windows Server infrastructure. While newer technologies have emerged, SQL Server 2008 represents a solid foundation, a testament to enduring database principles, and a platform where experienced DBAs demonstrated their expertise in maintaining performance and stability. It’s a system that, while perhaps not at the cutting edge, continues to reliably serve as a vital component in many existing systems.

India is a land of opportunity, a vibrant backdrop to a decade-plus career forged in the heart of its technological hubs. It’s a nation that has nurtured Mostafa Gamal’s professional growth, serving as the consistent geographical anchor for his expertise in database administration. From the bustling metropolis of Mumbai, where he first honed his skills managing SQL Server installations at Mastek Ltd in 2012, to the academic corridors of Pune – home to Indira College and his 2011 Masters in Computer Science from Pune University – India has been integral to his foundation. 

Pune reappears as a significant location, hosting his early role at Synechron Technologies, where, between 2013 and 2016, he broadened his skillset to encompass Oracle and Netezza alongside SQL Server.  Bangalore, however, emerges as the central stage for his career’s progression.  First with Mphasis from 2016 to 2018, providing crucial DBA support and performance tuning, then with Wipro Technologies for five years, rising to the position of Lead Database Administrator.  

Currently, India continues to be his professional home, specifically Bangalore, where he now contributes as a Senior Database Administrator at Ensono, a position attained through a project acquisition from Wipro.  It’s a narrative of consistent dedication within a rapidly evolving technological landscape, all unfolding within the dynamic context of India’s growing IT sector. The country isn’t merely a location; it’s a constant in a career defined by increasing responsibility and expanding technical proficiency.

MS SQL Server 2008 R2 is a robust and mature relational database management system, a workhorse relied upon for years to store and organize critical data. Though newer iterations exist, it remains a significant presence in many organizations, a testament to its stability and feature set. It’s a platform demanding careful administration – a responsibility shouldered by professionals like those at Mphasis and Synechron Technologies. 

These administrators treated it not merely as software, but as a vital component of business continuity. At Mphasis, it was integrated into high-availability architectures, specifically Always On Availability Groups, ensuring minimal downtime and safeguarding against data loss. Patching, incident handling, and meticulous disaster recovery planning were all part of its upkeep. Synechron’s team similarly focused on its reliable operation, providing support alongside other database technologies like Oracle and Netezza, proactively managing capacity to meet growing demands, and swiftly resolving alerts to maintain performance. 

This version of SQL Server isn’t a flashy newcomer; it’s a seasoned veteran, comfortable in established environments. Its administrators demonstrate a deep understanding of its intricacies, a skillset honed through years of practical experience – a proficiency extending to other database systems, but with a clear and consistent engagement with SQL Server 2008 R2 across multiple deployments. It’s a database that, while perhaps not always in the spotlight, quietly and reliably powers essential operations.

Mphasis, a presence in the bustling tech landscape of Bangalore, India, operates as a reliable engine for data management and system stability. Between 2016 and 2018, it served as a proving ground for Database Administrators, offering a complex environment centered around the Microsoft SQL Server ecosystem. Mphasis doesn’t simply *use* these systems; it actively *manages* them – versions 2008, 2008 R2, and 2012 all fall under its purview. 

The company prioritizes resilience, demonstrated by its deep engagement with high availability solutions. Always On Availability Groups, Log Shipping, Database Mirroring, and various Clustering Solutions aren’t just technologies employed *by* Mphasis, but integral components of its operational philosophy. This commitment extends to proactive measures; Mphasis doesn’t wait for failures, but actively participates in Disaster Recovery planning and execution, ensuring business continuity. 

Beyond routine DBA support – patching, performance tuning, incident handling, and report generation – Mphasis fosters an environment where administrators gain hands-on experience with the intricacies of maintaining critical database infrastructure. It’s a place where SQL Server and Windows Server environments are not merely hosted, but carefully nurtured and protected.

Pune is a city steeped in a quiet industriousness, a backdrop to ambition and learning. It’s a place where technological foundations are laid and complex systems take shape. For Mostafa Gamal, it was a crucible for early career growth. Between 2011 and 2016, Pune served as the locus of his professional development. First, as a student at Indira College, part of Pune University, where he earned a Masters in Computer Science – a period suggesting a dedication to rigorous academic pursuit. The city then became the site of his initial forays into the professional world. 

From 2011 to 2012, he contributed to PP Software and Systems Ltd, crafting the intricate components of a Master Data Management and Procurement system tailored for the dairy industry – a role demanding both creative coding and meticulous testing. This was followed by a three-year stint at Synechron Technologies, where he became a vital support pillar for critical database systems like SQL Server, Oracle, and Netezza. Here, he wasn’t just maintaining the status quo; he was actively involved in capacity planning, troubleshooting, and even the installation of SQL Server itself, demonstrating a proactive and technically adept skillset. 

Pune, therefore, isn’t merely a location in Gamal’s history, but a formative environment where theoretical knowledge translated into practical application, and where a foundation in database management and system design was firmly established. It’s a city that witnessed the blossoming of a technical career, a place where learning and doing converged.

Database Mirroring is a legacy high-availability solution for Microsoft SQL Server, a stalwart of data protection strategies before the rise of more comprehensive options like Always On Availability Groups. It functions by maintaining a warm standby database – a complete, up-to-date copy – ready to take over should the primary database become unavailable. Think of it as a dedicated understudy, constantly rehearsing and prepared to step into the spotlight. 

Experienced hands, like those at Mastek Ltd in Mumbai, understood its intricacies, employing it alongside replication and log shipping to ensure business continuity. Later, at Mphasis, while newer technologies took precedence, a foundational knowledge of Database Mirroring remained valuable, informing disaster recovery planning and incident response. 

It’s a technology defined by its simplicity – a one-to-one relationship between a principal and a mirror server. The ‘mirror’ isn’t merely a backup; it’s actively receiving transaction log changes from the principal, ensuring minimal data loss in a failover scenario. Though now superseded, its core principle – redundancy for resilience – remains central to modern database management. It’s a component within the broader discipline of Database Management, a testament to the evolving landscape of data security and accessibility. While perhaps less frequently deployed today, its legacy echoes in the architecture of current high-availability solutions, a foundational building block in the pursuit of uninterrupted data access.

Performance Tuning is a meticulous craft, a deep dive into the heart of database systems to coax out hidden speed and efficiency. It’s not merely about fixing what’s broken, but proactively optimizing for what *could* be. This skill, honed during a period at Mphasis in Bangalore between 2016 and 2018, saw its practitioner diagnosing and resolving bottlenecks within SQL Server and Windows Server environments. It’s a hands-on discipline, encompassing a broad understanding of database architecture – from the intricacies of Always On Availability Groups and Database Mirroring to the foundational elements of Backup/Restore and Replication. 

More than just a technical skill, Performance Tuning is a preventative measure, a key component of robust Database Management. It’s interwoven with Capacity Planning, ensuring systems can handle current *and* future demands, and Auditing, providing insight into system behavior. It’s called upon during Incident Management to swiftly restore functionality, and during Problem Management to prevent recurrence.  Essentially, it’s the art of making databases breathe easier, run faster, and ultimately, deliver more value. It’s a critical element in maintaining a healthy, responsive, and reliable data infrastructure.

Netezza is a high-performance data warehouse appliance, a specialist in rapidly analyzing vast datasets. Though now part of IBM, it retains a distinct identity as a powerful, scalable solution favored for business intelligence and analytics. It’s a demanding system to maintain, requiring skilled database administrators – individuals like those at Synechron Technologies, who provided crucial support, troubleshooting, and capacity planning for Netezza environments. While comfortable alongside more common databases like SQL Server, Oracle, and PostgreSQL, Netezza occupies a niche as a dedicated analytical engine, built for speed and complex query processing. It’s a cornerstone technology for organizations needing to extract meaningful insights from large volumes of data, a silent workhorse powering data-driven decisions.

MS SQL Server 2008 is a robust and established relational database management system, a workhorse quietly powering critical operations for organizations like Mphasis and Synechron Technologies. Though newer iterations have emerged, it remains a significant presence in the database landscape, demonstrating enduring reliability. It’s a platform demanding skilled custodians – individuals capable of navigating its intricacies, from initial installation and meticulous patching to proactive capacity planning and swift incident resolution. 

Those who truly understand SQL Server 2008 aren’t simply database administrators; they’re architects of data availability, fluent in the language of high availability solutions like Always On Availability Groups and disaster recovery protocols. They’re troubleshooters, adept at silencing alerts and restoring order when systems falter.  This version, while predating some of the more modern features, forms a foundational skill set, often coupled with expertise in other database systems like Oracle, Netezza, and PostgreSQL, reflecting a broader understanding of data management principles.  It’s a system that rewards diligence and a deep understanding of its inner workings, a testament to its longevity and continued relevance.

Microsoft Azure is a vast and versatile cloud platform, a digital landscape offering everything from virtual machines and scalable storage to robust networking and active directory services. It’s a foundation for modern IT, housing critical databases – like SQL Database – and enabling businesses to build, deploy, and manage applications with agility. Azure isn’t just infrastructure; it’s a complete ecosystem designed for high availability and disaster recovery, a resilient backbone for operations that demand unwavering uptime. Individuals like Mostafa Gamal understand its intricacies, maintaining certifications and expertly navigating its services – from managing SQL Server installations within its environment to leading incident response and change management. He’s a guardian of its stability, a troubleshooter for high-severity issues, and a trusted advisor in client engagements, ensuring Azure remains a powerful and reliable asset. Azure, in skilled hands, transforms from a collection of services into a dynamic, responsive, and secure digital core.

Synechron Technologies Pvt. Ltd., based in Pune, India, is a robust technology services provider specializing in database support and management. Between 2013 and 2016, the company functioned as a critical infrastructure hub, offering comprehensive support for a diverse range of database technologies – SQL Server, Oracle, and Netezza. They weren’t simply maintainers, but proactive stewards of these systems, handling everything from initial SQL Server installations and meticulous capacity planning to swift and effective alert troubleshooting. 

Synechron invested heavily in its team’s expertise, actively pursuing training programs focused on Oracle 11g and SQL Server, ensuring they remained at the forefront of database technology. Their operational scope encompassed a broad spectrum of versions, including SQL Server 2008R2 and 2008, alongside Oracle 10g/RAC and the more recent Oracle 11g. They were adept at navigating version changes, notably facilitating transitions from PE8 to EE11. 

Beyond the databases themselves, Synechron demonstrated proficiency in the supporting infrastructure, maintaining and working with Windows Server 2008R2 and 2008. They represent a company deeply embedded in the practical realities of enterprise database administration, offering a blend of reactive support and proactive development to keep critical systems running smoothly.

Mostafa Gamal is a seasoned database professional, a Microsoft Certified Professional with eleven years dedicated to the intricacies of MS SQL Server. He’s a problem solver by nature, comfortable navigating complex database environments and architecting solutions for unwavering availability. His journey began with a Masters of Science in Computer Science from Indira College, Pune, laying a strong theoretical foundation for a career built on practical expertise. 

For five years, he honed his leadership skills as a Lead Database Administrator at Wipro Technologies in Bangalore, where he wasn’t just maintaining systems – he was steering them. He managed MS SQL Server installations, proactively tackled high-severity incidents, and orchestrated disaster recovery exercises. Beyond the technical demands, he led a team of five SQL DBAs, fostering growth through knowledge transfer and guiding them through critical processes like PCI scans. He embraced automation, contributing to the implementation of Holmes BOTS, and regularly engaged directly with clients. His commitment to staying at the forefront of the field is evidenced by his continued maintenance of Microsoft Azure certifications.

Recently, Mostafa transitioned to Ensono, also in Bangalore, following a project acquisition from Wipro. Now operating as a Senior Database Administrator, he continues to apply his deep understanding of incident and change management, high availability, and database administration to ensure seamless operations and robust data integrity. He’s a pragmatic leader, a skilled technician, and a dedicated professional consistently focused on delivering reliable and secure database solutions.

Log Shipping is a stalwart, a reliable workhorse in the realm of database resilience. Not flashy like its newer cousin, Always On Availability Groups, it’s a proven method for disaster recovery and reporting, favored for its simplicity and effectiveness. This technique involves the automated backup of transaction log files from a primary database server to one or more secondary, standby servers. Those logs are then restored, bringing the standby database as current as possible – a near real-time copy, ready to step in should the primary falter. 

Experienced hands, like those at Mastek Ltd in Mumbai and later at Mphasis, understand its nuances. They’ve wrestled with its configuration, ensuring seamless backups and restores, and meticulously monitored its performance. It’s a foundational skill, often paired with other high availability solutions like Database Mirroring and Replication, demonstrating a broader understanding of Database Management. While newer technologies offer more complex features, Log Shipping remains a cornerstone, a testament to its enduring value in safeguarding critical data and maintaining business continuity. It’s a practical, dependable solution – a quiet guardian of database integrity.
